elements.note: Numbering template (<w:abstractNum>). Defines what a list LOOKS like — 9 levels, each with its own number format, marker text, indent, start value, and marker run properties. Reusable: many <w:num> instances can share one abstractNum. To apply the list to paragraphs, create a <w:num> pointing at this abstractNumId (--type num --prop abstractNumId=N), then reference that numId on the paragraph or style (--prop numId=N --prop ilvl=L). Top-level format/text/start/indent are shorthand for level0.*; per-level customization uses level<N>.* (N = 0..8). If a top-level format is set, every level inherits it instead of cycling through decimal/lowerLetter/lowerRoman.

abstractNumId.description: readback alias for id (matches the w:abstractNumId attribute name).

format.description: numFmt for level 0 (and propagated to all 9 levels if no per-level override). Common values: decimal, decimalZero, upperRoman, lowerRoman, upperLetter, lowerLetter, ordinal, cardinalText, ordinalText, bullet, chineseCounting, chineseLegalSimplified, japaneseCounting, koreanCounting, hex. Script-localized formats: arabicAlpha (أ ب ت ث), arabicAbjad (أ ب ج د), hebrew1, hebrew2, hindiNumbers, hindiVowels, hindiCounting, hindiConsonants, thaiCounting, thaiNumbers, thaiLetters. Top-level bare 'format' is shorthand for level0.format; setting it also defaults every other level to the same format instead of the decimal/lowerLetter/lowerRoman cycle.

id.description: abstractNumId (w:abstractNumId, unique within /numbering). Omit for auto-assignment. Renaming after Add would orphan every <w:num> referencing it; not supported.

indent.description: left indent in twips for level 0. Default (level+1)*720. Shorthand for level0.indent.

level<N>.font.description: marker font (rPr/rFonts on the level). Useful for bullet glyphs that need Symbol or Wingdings.

level<N>.format.description: numFmt for level N (N = 0..8). Same vocabulary as bare 'format'. Per-level override; only set the levels you actually use, the rest fall back to decimal/lowerLetter/lowerRoman cycle (or whatever 'format' propagates).

level<N>.hanging.description: hanging indent in twips for level N. Default 360.

level<N>.indent.description: left indent in twips for level N.

level<N>.justification.description: lvlJc — marker alignment within the indent area. Default left.

level<N>.size.description: marker font size (rPr/sz on the level). Bare number = pt.

level<N>.suff.description: what comes between the marker and the text content. Default tab.

level<N>.text.description: lvlText for level N. Same %N placeholder syntax.

name.description: human-readable name (w:name). Optional; Word shows this in the Numbering dialog.

numStyleLink.description: link to another abstractNum via numbering style (w:numStyleLink). Optional.

start.description: starting number for level 0. Default 1. Shorthand for level0.start.

styleLink.description: back-reference to a numbering style (w:styleLink). Optional.

text.description: lvlText for level 0. Use %N as placeholder for level N's running counter (e.g. '%1.', '%1.%2', '第%1章'). Bullet marker default: •. Top-level bare 'text' is shorthand for level0.text.

type.description: multiLevelType (w:multiLevelType). Default: hybridMultilevel. Hybrid is what Word writes for ad-hoc lists; singleLevel locks bullets/numbers to level 0.

elements.note: Main content container. Get returns the ordered stream of paragraphs, tables, sections. Mutate via child paths (/body/p[N], /body/tbl[N], /body/section[N]).

Bookmarks are BookmarkStart/End pairs. Name must be addressable: no whitespace, no '/[]"', no leading '@' or single quote. Duplicate names are allowed at Add (Word permits them; the bookmark id stays unique) and emit a warning — /bookmark[@name=NAME] then resolves to the first match.

id.description: OOXML bookmark id (w:bookmarkStart/@w:id). Assigned by the writer; surfaces only on Get/Query.
id.readback: numeric bookmark id as stored on BookmarkStart

text.description: optional bookmark-covered text. Without this, only an empty Start/End pair is inserted.
text.readback: not a distinct key — lives on wrapped runs

elements.note: Mirror of pptx/chart-axis. Axes are created/destroyed implicitly by chartType changes — no direct Add/Remove. At chart-creation time, configure via the chart's axis* props (axismin/axismax/axistitle/axisfont/…); chart-axis covers post-creation only. Known gaps: `labelFont` writes the title run (not tick labels); `lineWidth`/`lineDash` apply to all plot-area series — use chart-series for series-specific line styling.

At Add time, series pass as dotted props on the parent chart (series1.name, series1.values, series1.color, series1.categories); this schema is per-series Set/Get after creation. Combo charts (mixed chartType / secondary axis) are unsupported — create separate charts. `lineStyle` is not a key (rejected as UNSUPPORTED — use lineWidth + lineDash).

Embedded as inline DrawingML chart (c:chart) or extended chart (cx:chart) depending on chartType. Data via inline spec or per-series props. Mirrors pptx/chart surface. Axis configuration: chart-level axis* props (axismin, axismax, axistitle, axisfont, ...) are Add-time only; for post-creation axis Set/Get use the chart-axis element.

dispUnits.description: value-axis display units token readback (e.g. thousands, millions). Surfaces on the chart node when emitted by the value axis.

elements.note: Comments live in WordprocessingCommentsPart. Anchor: CommentRangeStart/End surround the target run or paragraph; CommentReference marks the inline anchor.

anchoredTo.description: path of the paragraph or run the comment is anchored to (resolved from CommentRangeStart).

id.description: OOXML comment id (w:comment/@w:id). Assigned by the writer; surfaces only on Get/Query.

range.description: whether the comment spans a text range (default true: emits <w:commentRangeStart/> + <w:commentRangeEnd/> around the anchor plus a <w:commentReference/> run). Pass range=false for a zero-width / point-anchored comment that carries ONLY <w:commentReference/> — used by dump→batch to preserve reference-only comments without synthesizing a spurious range.

runStart.description: 1-based run index inside the parent paragraph where the comment range starts. N=0 (default) anchors the comment to paragraph start; N>=1 places <w:commentRangeStart/> immediately after the Nth run so dump→batch round-trip restores intra-paragraph anchor positions.
runStart.readback: n/a (encoded inline; surfaces via anchoredTo path)

elements.note: Root container. Get returns top-level children (body, styles, numbering, headers, footers, etc.). Set exposes core document properties (author/title/subject/keywords/description).

autoSpaceDE.description: auto-spacing between East Asian and Latin text.

autoSpaceDN.description: auto-spacing between East Asian and numeric text.

bookFoldPrintingSheets.description: settings.xml bookFoldPrintingSheets — sheets per booklet signature when book-fold printing.

bookFoldReversePrinting.description: settings.xml bookFoldRevPrinting flag — true when book-fold printing reverses the page order.

category.description: document category metadata. Emitted only when present.

columns.equalWidth.description: document-default columns equal-width flag (sectPr cols @equalWidth on the body sectPr).

columns.separator.description: document-default columns separator flag (sectPr cols @sep on the body sectPr).

created.description: creation timestamp (ISO-8601). Emitted only when present.

doNotDisplayPageBoundaries.description: settings.xml doNotDisplayPageBoundaries flag — Word view hides the page-boundary frame.

locale.description: primary document locale derived from theme themeFontLang (e.g. 'en-US', 'zh-CN', 'ar-SA'). Read-only summary — change via run lang.* slots or theme themeFontLang.

marginBottom.description: convenience readback from sectPr; primary edit path is /section[N].

marginFooter.description: footer-from-bottom-edge distance (pgMar @footer). Convenience readback from sectPr; primary edit path is /section[N].

marginGutter.description: binding gutter width (pgMar @gutter). Convenience readback from sectPr; primary edit path is /section[N].

marginHeader.description: header-from-top-edge distance (pgMar @header). Convenience readback from sectPr; primary edit path is /section[N].

marginLeft.description: convenience readback from sectPr; primary edit path is /section[N].

marginRight.description: convenience readback from sectPr; primary edit path is /section[N].

marginTop.description: convenience readback from sectPr; primary edit path is /section[N].

modified.description: last modification timestamp (ISO-8601). Emitted only when present.

orientation.description: convenience readback from sectPr; primary edit path is /section[N].

pageHeight.description: convenience readback from sectPr; primary edit path is /section[N].

pageWidth.description: convenience readback from sectPr; primary edit path is /section[N].

revisionNumber.description: document save counter from docProps/core.xml (cp:revision). Distinct from tracked-change revisions — see `help docx revision`.

sectPr.description: dump→batch round-trip marker: forces the body's final section to emit a (possibly empty) <w:sectPr/> so an empty source sectPr is not dropped. Internal round-trip use; not an interactive edit.

elements.note: Endnotes live in EndnotesPart. Semantics mirror footnote.json. Parent must be a paragraph path (/body/p[N]); use --index N to control position within the paragraph. Run-level parent (/body/p[N]/r[M]) is not accepted -- the endnote reference is inserted as a new run.

align.description: Horizontal alignment applied to the endnote content paragraph (<w:jc/>).

bold.cs.description: complex-script bold for the endnote's runs (<w:bCs/>). Required for Arabic / Hebrew bold rendering.

direction.description: Reading direction. 'rtl' writes <w:bidi/> on the endnote content paragraph and cascades <w:rtl/> to the paragraph mark.

font.ea.description: East-Asian font slot (rFonts/eastAsia) — Chinese / Japanese / Korean typefaces.

font.latin.description: Latin font slots (rFonts/ascii + hAnsi) — ASCII / Western text.

id.description: OOXML endnote id; source of @endnoteId in stable path /endnote[@endnoteId=N].

italic.cs.description: complex-script italic (<w:iCs/>) for the endnote's runs.

size.cs.description: complex-script font size (<w:szCs/>) for the endnote's runs.

elements.note: Aliases: formula, math. formula input is parsed by FormulaParser (LaTeX-ish). Display mode wraps in oMathPara; inline mode appends oMath to the parent paragraph. Get returns only `mode` (display|inline) in Format[]; the formula source itself is in DocumentNode.Text.

Complex field (fldChar: begin/instr/separate/result/end), addressed as a whole at /field[N]; /body/p[N]/r[M] returns the inner fieldChar run, not the field. Per-type required props: mergefield/ref → name (aka fieldName/bookmarkName); seq → identifier; styleref → styleName; docproperty → propertyName; if → expression (+ optional trueText/falseText); date/time accept optional format.

evaluated.description: cross-handler protocol flag — true when the field has a cached result run that the opening application can display, false when the field was written without a cached value (view text substitutes #OCLI_NOTEVAL!{instr}; view issues emits subtype field_not_evaluated). For dirty cached fields the cache is still trusted by evaluated; staleness surfaces separately via subtype field_cache_stale. Read this via get --json instead of pattern-matching the sentinel, since multiple fields per paragraph and literal user content may collide.

expression.description: IF field's logical expression (e.g. 'MERGEFIELD Gender = "Male"'). Add/Set only — surfaces back inside the `instruction` readback, not as its own Format key.

falseText.description: IF field's text shown when expression evaluates false. Add/Set only — surfaces back inside the `instruction` readback.

fldLock.description: field lock (w:fldChar/w:fldSimple @w:fldLock) — when true, Word does NOT update the field on F9 / Update Field / recalc; the cached result stays put. Universal across field types (rides on the begin fldChar). On a dump→batch round-trip a fldSimple lock is preserved through the conversion to a complex field.
fldLock.readback: true (only emitted when the field is locked)

format.description: field format — bare picture string (e.g. 'yyyy-MM-dd' for DATE, '0.00%' for numeric). The handler wraps it into the OOXML '\@ "..."' switch automatically; do NOT pass the '\@' prefix yourself.

hyperlink.description: REF field: append \h switch so the inserted reference becomes a clickable hyperlink to the bookmark target. Add/Set only — surfaces back as a switch inside the `instruction` readback.

id.description: SEQ field's identifier (sequence label). Defaults to 'name' when 'id' is not supplied. Alias: identifier.

instruction.description: Raw field instruction text. Bypasses fieldType-specific helpers — useful for arbitrary fields not covered by the typed shortcuts.

name.description: Per-type identifier: mergefield → field name (e.g. CustomerName); ref/pageref/noteref → target bookmark name; styleref → style name; docproperty → property name. Aliases preserve historical naming differences (e.g. ref docs called this 'bookmarkName').

trueText.description: IF field's text shown when expression evaluates true. Add/Set only — surfaces back inside the `instruction` readback.

vertAlign.description: field-wide vertical alignment applied uniformly to every run of the field (begin/instrText/separate/result/end). Common case: a superscript cross-reference citation mark ([1],[2]…). Accepts the boolean aliases superscript=true / subscript=true. Like the other run-format props the field add accepts (font/size/bold/color), this rides on the whole field rather than a single run.
vertAlign.readback: n/a (applied to the field's run properties)

elements.note: Field-character marker (w:fldChar) — inline atom that delimits a complex field's begin / separate / end boundaries. Atomic add is intentionally NOT supported because a fldChar in isolation is invalid OOXML; use --type field to insert a complete begin+instrText+separate+cached+end sequence as one unit. Get/Set on individual fldChars allows audit→fix workflows to inspect and adjust an existing field's structure.

Mirror of header.json — same surface, stored in FooterParts. Duplicate type per section rejected at Add. A single Add supports at most one text + one field pair. For composite footers like 'Page X of Y' (two fields + literal text), create the footer first, then Add additional runs/fields to its paragraph (/footer[N]/p[1]) one by one — see examples.

bold.readback: true when bold, key absent otherwise

color.description: font color. Accepts #RRGGBB, RRGGBB, named colors (red, blue…), rgb(r,g,b), or 3-char shorthand (F00).

direction.description: Reading direction. 'rtl' writes <w:bidi/> on the footer paragraph, <w:rtl/> on the paragraph mark, and <w:rtl/> on every run (text + field runs alike) so Arabic / Hebrew character order reverses end-to-end. 'ltr' clears all three.

field.readback: not surfaced as a distinct key

italic.readback: true when italic, key absent otherwise

size.description: font size. Accepts bare number or pt-suffixed.

elements.note: Footnotes live in FootnotesPart. A FootnoteReference run is inserted at the anchor point; the note body is appended to footnotes.xml. Parent must be a paragraph path (/body/p[N]); use --index N to control position within the paragraph. Run-level parent (/body/p[N]/r[M]) is not accepted -- the footnote reference is inserted as a new run.

align.description: Horizontal alignment applied to the footnote content paragraph (<w:jc/>).

bold.cs.description: complex-script bold (<w:bCs/>). Required for Arabic / Hebrew bold rendering.

direction.description: Reading direction. 'rtl' writes <w:bidi/> on the footnote content paragraph and cascades <w:rtl/> to the paragraph mark.

id.description: OOXML footnote id (w:footnote/@w:id). Assigned by the writer; surfaces only on Get/Query.

italic.cs.description: complex-script italic (<w:iCs/>) for the footnote's runs.

size.cs.description: complex-script font size (<w:szCs/>) for the footnote's runs.

elements.note: Form fields embed a BookmarkStart/End so names share the bookmark namespace and validation rules. Three kinds: text (default), checkbox, dropdown.

default.description: form-field default value. Text-fields surface the default string; dropdowns surface the integer default index.

enabled.description: true when the form field accepts user input (FFData @enabled). Defaults true when the element is absent.

hasFormFieldData.description: true when the run carries an embedded fldData payload (legacy form-field binary blob).

name.description: form field name (required for stable addressing). Same constraints as bookmark name.

text.description: initial text value (text fields only). Alias: value.

elements.note: Headers are stored in HeaderParts and referenced by the last sectPr. Duplicate type rejected at Add. 'first' type auto-enables TitlePage. Field insertion uses complex fldChar (begin/instr/separate/result/end). A single Add supports at most one text + one field pair; composite headers like 'Page X of Y' must be built in steps by adding additional runs/fields to the header's paragraph (/header[N]/p[1]) after creation — see examples.

bold.readback: true when bold, key absent otherwise

color.description: font color. Accepts #RRGGBB, RRGGBB, named colors (red, blue…), rgb(r,g,b), or 3-char shorthand (F00).

direction.description: Reading direction. 'rtl' writes <w:bidi/> on the header paragraph, <w:rtl/> on the paragraph mark, and <w:rtl/> on every run (text + field runs alike) so Arabic / Hebrew character order reverses end-to-end. 'ltr' clears all three.

field.description: complex field to insert (page/numpages/date/author/title/time/filename, or an arbitrary field name).
field.readback: not surfaced as a distinct key

italic.readback: true when italic, key absent otherwise

size.description: font size. Accepts bare number or pt-suffixed.

elements.note: Aliases: link. Exactly one of 'url' (external) or 'anchor' (internal bookmark ref) is required. Colors default to theme Hyperlink (or 0563C1 fallback) with single underline.

anchor.description: bookmark name for internal links. Set after Add not supported — formatting lives on inner runs (Set the run, not the hyperlink wrapper).

bold.description: Set after Add not supported — formatting lives on inner runs (Set the run, not the hyperlink wrapper).

color.description: override link color (default: theme Hyperlink or 0563C1). Set after Add not supported — formatting lives on inner runs (Set the run, not the hyperlink wrapper).
color.readback: #-prefixed uppercase hex, or scheme color name (e.g. 'hyperlink', 'followedhyperlink') when using theme default

docLocation.description: w:docLocation — frame name (or location-within-document for non-text-anchor refs). Preserved on dump round-trip. Get only; set via the underlying hyperlink rewrite path on Add.

font.description: Set after Add not supported — formatting lives on inner runs (Set the run, not the hyperlink wrapper).

italic.description: Set after Add not supported — formatting lives on inner runs (Set the run, not the hyperlink wrapper).

rStyle.description: Run style id applied to the hyperlink's run (typically 'Hyperlink' to inherit theme color/underline).

size.description: Set after Add not supported — formatting lives on inner runs (Set the run, not the hyperlink wrapper).

text.description: display text. Defaults to url or anchor value if omitted.

underline.description: underline style for the link's run. Defaults to single when omitted (theme Hyperlink look). Set after Add not supported — formatting lives on inner runs (Set the run, not the hyperlink wrapper). Aliases: font.underline.

url.description: external URL. Aliases: href, link. docx Set accepts all three (url canonical).

elements.note: Field-instruction text (w:instrText) — the body of a complex field that holds the instruction string (e.g. 'PAGE \\* MERGEFORMAT', 'DATE \\@ "yyyy-MM-dd"'). Atomic add is intentionally NOT supported because instrText outside a field is invalid; use --type field to insert a complete sequence. Set is supported so audit→fix workflows can rewrite a field's instruction (e.g. PAGE → DATE) without touching the surrounding fldChar markers.

instruction.description: The Word field instruction. Leading/trailing spaces inside the value are significant — they form the OOXML separator between switches. Alias: instr.

elements.note: Single <w:lvl> under an <w:abstractNum>. L is the level index (w:ilvl, 0..8). 'add abstractNum' pre-populates 9 default levels (lvl%3 cycle); adding another --type level with the same ilvl replaces it in place. Aliases lvl / level. Top-level abstractNum.* properties also accept level0.* dotted forms for level-0 customization at creation time — this element is for adjusting levels 1..8 or replacing levels post-Add.

direction.description: right-to-left direction on the level's pPr. Only 'rtl' writes <w:bidi/>; 'ltr' is the canonical clear.

font.description: marker font (rPr/rFonts). Useful for bullet glyphs that need Symbol or Wingdings.

format.description: numFmt for this level. Common values: decimal, decimalZero, upperRoman, lowerRoman, upperLetter, lowerLetter, ordinal, cardinalText, ordinalText, bullet, chineseCounting, chineseLegalSimplified, japaneseCounting, koreanCounting, hex. Default decimal.

indent.description: left indent in twips (pPr/ind/left). On Add, default (ilvl+1)*720.

isLgl.description: w:isLgl — render this level's counter as decimal regardless of numFmt (legal-style numbering).

justification.description: lvlJc — marker alignment within the indent area. Default left.

lvlRestart.description: w:lvlRestart — level at which to restart this counter (0 = never restart).

lvlText.description: lvlText template. Use %N to insert level N's running counter (e.g. '%1.', '%1.%2', '第%1章'). Default: %{ilvl+1}. for ordered, • for bullet.

size.description: marker font size (rPr/sz). Bare number = pt.

suff.description: separator between the marker and the text content. Default tab.

elements.note: Numbering instance (<w:num>). A thin pointer that references one <w:abstractNum> via abstractNumId — the abstractNum is the template, the num is the live counter. Paragraphs reference 'numId' (this element's id), not abstractNumId. Two modes: (B/C) --prop abstractNumId=N reuses an existing template; (A) --prop format=... auto-creates a matching abstractNum on the fly. By default each new num gets a startOverride on level 0 so two instances of the same abstractNum count independently — pass --prop continue=true to inherit Word's literal counter-continuation behavior instead.

abstractNumId.description: abstractNum to reuse (mode B/C). Must already exist in /numbering. Mutually exclusive with mode-A format-based auto-create. Set to mutate after creation.

continue.description: if true, suppress the default level-0 startOverride injection — this num inherits Word's literal counter-continuation behavior from the abstractNum (two num instances on the same abstractNum keep counting together).

format.description: mode A: when no abstractNumId is supplied, build a matching abstractNum from these props (format/text/indent/type/start) and bind this num to it. Same vocabulary as abstractNum.format.

id.description: numId (w:numId, unique within /numbering). Omit for auto-assignment. Paragraphs reference this id via --prop numId=N.

indent.description: mode A: left indent (twips) for the auto-created abstractNum level 0.

numId.description: readback alias for id (matches the w:numId attribute name).

start.description: starting number for level 0. Shorthand for startOverride.0. Default behavior: a fresh num auto-injects a startOverride on level 0 (using the abstractNum's level-0 start, typically 1) so independent instances of the same template don't share counters. Pass --prop continue=true to suppress this.

startOverride.<N>.description: per-level startOverride (N = 0..8). Emits <w:lvlOverride w:ilvl=N><w:startOverride w:val=...>. Use to reset specific levels without disturbing others.

text.description: mode A: lvlText for the auto-created abstractNum level 0.

type.description: mode A: multiLevelType for the auto-created abstractNum.

elements.note: NumberingDefinitionsPart container — bullet / numbered list definitions. The container itself is read-only; mutate via its children: --type abstractNum (the template) and --type num (the instance), see docx/abstractNum.json and docx/num.json. Paragraphs/styles then bind to an instance by id via the 'numId' / 'ilvl' props, or via the higher-level 'listStyle' shortcut (see docx/paragraph.json).

abstractNumCount.description: total number of abstractNum definitions in numbering.xml.

abstractNumId.description: per-num child readback — the abstractNumId referenced by each /num child. Surfaces on enumerated num child nodes, not the numbering container itself.

elements.note: Aliases: oleobject, object, embed. Embeds a binary package plus a preview image. Source accepted as file path, URL, or data-URI.

Inline w:br element wrapped in a run. Aliases: columnbreak, break. type=column → column break; type=page (default) → page break; type=line/textWrapping → soft line break. Surfaced by Get as type=break (when the run carries no <w:t> alongside the <w:br>).

breakType.description: Canonical key on Get/Set. Add accepts 'type' as a parallel synonym for symmetry with the historical alias.

type.description: Add-only alias; Get surfaces this as 'breakType', and Set requires 'breakType'.

elements.note: Get returns canonical keys: spaceBefore/spaceAfter/lineSpacing/align (legacy aliases spacebefore/linespacing/halign still accepted on Add/Set). effective.* keys are read-only values resolved through paragraph style → docDefaults from the first run; each carries an effective.X.src pointer to the writing layer (e.g. /styles/Heading1). Suppressed when the paragraph (or first run) sets the direct value.

bold.description: run-level bold. On Add, applied to the implicit run created by 'text'. On Set, applied to all runs in the paragraph and to the paragraph-mark run properties so subsequent runs inherit.

bold.cs.description: complex-script bold for the paragraph's runs (<w:bCs/>). Required for Arabic / Hebrew bold rendering.

caps.description: run-level all caps. On Add only (no paragraph-level Set wrapper).

charSpacing.description: run-level character spacing in points (bare number = pt, or 'Xpt'). Stored as twips. On Add only.

color.description: run-level text color. Accepts #RRGGBB, RRGGBB, named colors (e.g. red), rgb(r,g,b).

contextualSpacing.description: suppress space between paragraphs of the same style. Applied on Add/Set to paragraph pPr; also valid on Style.

direction.description: paragraph reading direction. 'rtl' writes <w:bidi/> on pPr, <w:rtl/> on the paragraph mark, and <w:rtl/> on every run (so Arabic / Hebrew character order reverses inside runs, not just page-side layout). 'ltr' clears all three.
direction.readback: rtl | ltr (only emitted when explicitly set)

dstrike.description: run-level double strikethrough. On Add only.

effective.color.description: inheritance-resolved font color (read-only). #RRGGBB or scheme color name.

effective.rtl.description: inheritance-resolved right-to-left flag (read-only). Emitted even when 'rtl' is set directly so callers can compare direct vs cascade-resolved state.

effective.size.description: inheritance-resolved font size (read-only) — derived from the first run's style chain → paragraph style → docDefaults.

emboss.description: run-level emboss text effect. On Add only.

firstLineChars.description: first-line indent in 1/100 character units (CT_Ind @firstLineChars). Word's chars-relative variant of firstLineIndent.

font.description: run-level font family (applied to Ascii/HighAnsi/EastAsia). On Add, applied to the implicit run created by 'text'. On Set, applied to all runs in the paragraph.

font.asciiTheme.description: Theme font binding for the ascii slot (rFonts/asciiTheme). Values: minorHAnsi, majorHAnsi, minorEastAsia, majorEastAsia, minorBidi, majorBidi.

font.cs.description: Complex-script font slot (rFonts/cs) — Arabic / Hebrew / Thai typefaces.

font.csTheme.description: Theme font binding for the complex-script slot (rFonts/cstheme). Values: minorBidi, majorBidi.

font.ea.description: East-Asian font slot (rFonts/eastAsia) — Chinese / Japanese / Korean typefaces.

font.eaTheme.description: Theme font binding for the East-Asia slot (rFonts/eastAsiaTheme). Values: minorEastAsia, majorEastAsia.

font.hAnsiTheme.description: Theme font binding for the hAnsi slot (rFonts/hAnsiTheme).

font.latin.description: Latin font slots (rFonts/ascii + hAnsi) — ASCII / Western text.

hangingChars.description: hanging indent in 1/100 character units (CT_Ind @hangingChars). Word's chars-relative variant of hangingIndent.

hangingIndent.description: hanging indent (pairs with left indent). Routed through SpacingConverter.

highlight.description: run-level highlight color (w:highlight values: yellow, green, cyan, magenta, blue, red, darkBlue, darkCyan, darkGreen, darkMagenta, darkRed, darkYellow, darkGray, lightGray, black, white, none).

imprint.description: run-level imprint (engrave) text effect. On Add only.

italic.cs.description: complex-script italic (<w:iCs/>) for the paragraph's runs.

keepLines.description: keep all lines of the paragraph together (no page break within).

keepNext.description: keep paragraph with the next paragraph (no page break between).

kern.description: kerning threshold on an empty paragraph's ¶-mark run (w:pPr/w:rPr/w:kern @val, half-points). Below this font size Word disables kerning. Get-side readback only — the value is applied via the generic run-formatting path (ApplyRunFormatting), not a dedicated paragraph Set case; text-bearing paragraphs surface the ¶-mark kern under markRPr.kern instead.

lineRule.description: line spacing rule paired with lineSpacing (Word-only: w:spacing@w:lineRule). 'auto' = multiplier (default for 1.5x/150%), 'exact' = exact fixed height (default for Npt), 'atLeast' = minimum height (Npt floor; lines may grow to fit tall content).

listStyle.description: high-level list type. 'bullet' (aliases: unordered, ul) creates a bulleted list; 'ordered' (or any other non-bullet value, e.g. 'decimal') creates a numbered list; 'none'/'remove'/'clear' strips list formatting. Preferred over raw numId. Continues a preceding list of the same type automatically unless 'start' is also given.

markRPr.bold.description: paragraph-mark run property — bold flag on the ¶ glyph (w:pPr/w:rPr/w:b). Distinct from per-run bold; affects how the mark itself renders and is inherited by appended runs without their own bold setting.

markRPr.bold.cs.description: paragraph-mark run complex-script bold (w:pPr/w:rPr/w:bCs). The CJK/RTL counterpart of markRPr.bold.

markRPr.charSpacing.description: paragraph-mark run character spacing (w:pPr/w:rPr/w:spacing @val, twips internally).

markRPr.color.description: paragraph-mark run color (w:pPr/w:rPr/w:color). Returns scheme color name for theme refs, or #-prefixed hex.

markRPr.font.ascii.description: paragraph-mark run Ascii font (w:pPr/w:rPr/w:rFonts @ascii). Emitted alongside markRPr.font.hAnsi only when the two slots diverge; the symmetric case collapses to markRPr.font.latin.

markRPr.font.asciiTheme.description: paragraph-mark run theme font binding (w:pPr/w:rPr/w:rFonts/@w:asciiTheme), e.g. minorHAnsi.

markRPr.font.csTheme.description: paragraph-mark run theme font binding (w:pPr/w:rPr/w:rFonts/@w:csTheme), e.g. minorHAnsi.

markRPr.font.eaTheme.description: paragraph-mark run theme font binding (w:pPr/w:rPr/w:rFonts/@w:eastAsiaTheme), e.g. minorHAnsi.

markRPr.font.hAnsi.description: paragraph-mark run HighAnsi font (w:pPr/w:rPr/w:rFonts @hAnsi). Emitted alongside markRPr.font.ascii only when the two slots diverge; the symmetric case collapses to markRPr.font.latin.

markRPr.font.hAnsiTheme.description: paragraph-mark run theme font binding (w:pPr/w:rPr/w:rFonts/@w:hAnsiTheme), e.g. minorHAnsi.

markRPr.font.hint.description: paragraph-mark run font slot hint (w:pPr/w:rPr/w:rFonts @hint) — eastAsia / cs / default; selects the font slot for ambiguous characters on the paragraph mark.

markRPr.italic.cs.description: paragraph-mark run complex-script italic (w:pPr/w:rPr/w:iCs). The CJK/RTL counterpart of markRPr.italic.

markRPr.kern.description: paragraph-mark run kerning threshold (w:pPr/w:rPr/w:kern @val, half-points). Below this font size Word disables kerning on the ¶ glyph.

markRPr.lang.cs.description: paragraph-mark run complex-script language tag (w:pPr/w:rPr/w:lang @bidi, BCP-47 e.g. ar-SA). Mirrors run-level lang.cs.

markRPr.lang.ea.description: paragraph-mark run East-Asian language tag (w:pPr/w:rPr/w:lang @eastAsia, BCP-47 e.g. ja-JP). Mirrors run-level lang.ea.

markRPr.lang.latin.description: paragraph-mark run Latin language tag (w:pPr/w:rPr/w:lang @val, BCP-47 e.g. en-US). Tags the ¶ glyph's language for proofing; mirrors run-level lang.latin.

markRPr.rStyle.description: paragraph-mark run character style (w:pPr/w:rPr/w:rStyle). Emitted on Get when the style binds the mark only; bare rStyle covers the mark+implicit-run shape.

markRPr.size.description: paragraph-mark run font size (w:pPr/w:rPr/w:sz, half-points internally).

markRPr.size.cs.description: paragraph-mark run complex-script font size (w:pPr/w:rPr/w:szCs, half-points internally). The CJK/RTL counterpart of markRPr.size; emitted separately so the ¶ glyph's complex-script metrics survive dump→batch.

noproof.description: run-level no-proofing flag. On Add only.

numFmt.description: raw numbering format (e.g. bullet, decimal, lowerLetter). Emitted only when present.

numId.description: numbering definition id (w:numId). Low-level entry point — prefer 'listStyle' unless you specifically need to reference an existing numbering instance. Requires the numId to already exist in /numbering (create via `add /numbering --type num` first).
numId.readback: numbering id as stored on the paragraph

numLevel.description: list indent level (w:ilvl), 0..8. Requires numId or listStyle to be effective; Get only surfaces numLevel when numId is present on the paragraph.
numLevel.readback: integer level as stored on the paragraph (only when numId is set)

outline.description: run-level outline text effect. On Add only.

outlineLvl.description: outline level (0-9). Used by Word's TOC and document map.

pageBreakBefore.description: force a page break before this paragraph.

paraId.description: paragraph stable id (source of @paraId in stable path). Emitted only when present.

pbdr.bar.description: paragraph border edge descriptor. Emitted only when present.
pbdr.bar.readback: n/a (handler does not surface paragraph borders today)

pbdr.between.description: paragraph border edge descriptor. Emitted only when present.
pbdr.between.readback: n/a (handler does not surface paragraph borders today)

pbdr.bottom.description: paragraph border edge descriptor. Emitted only when present.
pbdr.bottom.readback: n/a (handler does not surface paragraph borders today)

pbdr.left.description: paragraph border edge descriptor. Emitted only when present.
pbdr.left.readback: n/a (handler does not surface paragraph borders today)

pbdr.right.description: paragraph border edge descriptor. Emitted only when present.
pbdr.right.readback: n/a (handler does not surface paragraph borders today)

pbdr.top.description: paragraph border edge descriptor. Emitted only when present.
pbdr.top.readback: n/a (handler does not surface paragraph borders today)

rStyle.description: Paragraph mark run style id (e.g. FootnoteReference, IntenseEmphasis). Inherited by runs without their own rStyle.

rtl.description: run-level right-to-left text flag. On Add only.

shading.val.description: shading pattern value (decomposed from `shd`). Add/Set use `shd`.

shadow.description: run-level shadow text effect. On Add only.

shd.description: shading. Format: 'fill' or 'val;fill' or 'val;fill;color'. Applied at paragraph level on Add (pPr/shd).

size.description: run-level font size. Accepts bare number (pt), '14pt', '10.5pt'.

size.cs.description: complex-script font size (<w:szCs/>) for the paragraph's runs.

smallcaps.description: run-level small caps. On Add only.

spaceAfterAuto.description: w:spacing @afterAutospacing — automatic spacing toggle for the space AFTER the paragraph. See spaceBeforeAuto.

spaceBeforeAuto.description: w:spacing @beforeAutospacing — Word's 'automatic spacing between paragraphs of the same style' toggle for the space BEFORE the paragraph. Surfaced on get only when present.

start.description: starting number for an ordered list (w:start on level 0 of the numbering definition). Only meaningful together with liststyle=ordered or an existing numid. Readback not implemented — w:start lives in the separate numbering part and cross-part traversal is fragile; query the numbering directly if you need it.
start.readback: n/a (write-only via paragraph; query numbering part)

style.description: paragraph styleId (e.g. Heading1, Normal, Quote). Must reference an existing style or one of the built-in style aliases. Aliases mirror the canonical readback keys exposed by Get: styleId targets the OOXML styleId; styleName resolves the display name through the styles part (lenient, falls back to verbatim if no match).
style.readback: styleId as stored on the paragraph

subscript.description: run-level subscript vertical alignment. On Add only (use vertAlign for Set).

superscript.description: run-level superscript vertical alignment. On Add only (use vertAlign for Set).

tabs.description: tab stops array. Set accepts a comma-separated list of positions (twips, e.g. 720,1440); Get emits the stored tab stops when present.

underline.description: run-level underline. Accepts 'true'/'false' or an underline style (single, double, thick, dotted, dash, wavy, etc.).

underline.color.description: underline stroke color, written to w:u/@w:color. Preserved when 'underline' style is toggled.

vanish.description: run-level hidden text. On Add only.

vertAlign.description: run-level vertical text alignment. On Add only.

wordWrap.description: Latin-word break behaviour in CJK paragraphs. Set false to allow ASCII text/whitespace to participate in CJK character flow — required for right-aligned CJK lines that rely on trailing underlined whitespace to align with adjacent lines.

elements.note: Ranged editing-permission markers. <w:permStart>/<w:permEnd> delimit a region a group (edGrp) or specific user (ed) may edit inside a protected document. Positioned paragraph children like bookmark markers: permStart opens the region, permEnd (type=permEnd, id only) closes the matching id. Use a shared id to pair them. colFirst/colLast scope the permission to a table-column range.

colFirst.description: first table column (0-based) the permission applies to, for a table-column range.

colLast.description: last table column (0-based) the permission applies to, for a table-column range.

ed.description: specific user (e.g. domain\user or email) allowed to edit the region.

edGrp.description: editing group allowed in the region (ST_EdGrp).

id.description: marker id (required). permStart and its matching permEnd share the same id.

elements.note: Aliases: image, img. 'src' is required (alias 'path'). Image source resolved via ImageSource — accepts file path, URL, data-URI, or raw bytes. SVGs auto-generate a PNG fallback.

anchor.description: true to create a floating (anchored) picture instead of inline. Required to enable wrap/hPosition/vPosition/hRelative/vRelative/behindText.

behindText.description: true when the picture floats behind text (anchor @behindDoc=1).

crop.description: Crop rectangle in percent (0-100), 1 or 4 comma-separated values (left,top,right,bottom). Stored as <a:srcRect> inside <a:blipFill> (1000ths-of-a-percent). Get emits the canonical 4-value form (e.g. 12,6,18,9); the per-side keys (cropLeft/cropTop/cropRight/cropBottom) are accepted on add/set only.

cropBottom.description: Crop from the bottom edge as percent (0-100). Set/add only — Get folds all sides into the canonical crop key.

cropLeft.description: Crop from the left edge as percent (0-100). Set/add only — Get folds all sides into the canonical crop key.

cropRight.description: Crop from the right edge as percent (0-100). Set/add only — Get folds all sides into the canonical crop key.

cropTop.description: Crop from the top edge as percent (0-100). Set/add only — Get folds all sides into the canonical crop key.

effectExtent.description: drawing visual overflow/effect margin (wp:effectExtent) as 4 comma-separated EMU ints: left,top,right,bottom. Word's inline-picture layout height depends on this even when wp:extent is identical, so the dump→batch round-trip restores it byte-for-byte. Round-trip prop — emitted by `dump --format batch`, not by `get`; absent value defaults to 0,0,0,0 (the interactive Add default).

hAlign.description: relative horizontal alignment keyword for a floating picture (left, center, right, inside, outside) via positionH/align. Overrides hPosition when set.

hPosition.description: absolute horizontal anchor position in cm (positionH/posOffset).

hRelative.description: horizontal anchor reference frame (e.g. page, margin, column, character).

height.description: height — cm length (extent.Cy/Cx in EMU formatted to cm).

link.description: click-hyperlink target for the image, making it clickable (<a:hlinkClick> on pic:cNvPr). An absolute URL or relative target round-trips as a TargetMode=External relationship; a '#anchor' or bare bookmark name round-trips as an internal anchor.
link.readback: resolved external URL, or internal anchor name

relativeHeight.description: z-order (stacking) of an anchored/floating picture (anchor @relativeHeight). Higher = nearer the front. Distinct per overlapping float; round-trips the raw OOXML value.
relativeHeight.readback: raw uint z-order on floating pictures (e.g. `251664384`)

vAlign.description: relative vertical alignment keyword for a floating picture (top, bottom, center, inside, outside) via positionV/align. Overrides vPosition when set.

vPosition.description: absolute vertical anchor position in cm (positionV/posOffset).

vRelative.description: vertical anchor reference frame (e.g. page, margin, paragraph, line).

width.description: width — cm length (extent.Cy/Cx in EMU formatted to cm).

wrap.description: text wrapping mode for floating picture (none, square, tight, topandbottom, through). For 'behind text', use wrap=none + behindText=true.

elements.note: Inline positional tab (w:ptab, Word 2007+). Anchors left/center/right alignment regions in headers/footers. Inserted as <w:r><w:ptab/></w:r>; surfaced by Get as type=ptab. Aliases: positionaltab.

Tracked-change marker. `query revision` enumerates every w:ins / w:del / w:moveFrom / w:moveTo / w:*PrChange in the document. The synthetic Path is `/revision[@id=N]` when the marker carries a stable w:id (true for everything Word/WPS writes), falling back to positional `/revision[N]` only for markers without an id — same precedence as paragraphs (`p[@paraId=…]` → `p[N]`). When the same w:id is shared by more than one marker (canonical case: a moveFrom/moveTo pair binds via shared id), the Path is disambiguated as `/revision[@id=N][@type=moveFrom]` / `[@type=moveTo]` so single-end addressing stays unique. `set /revision[@id=N]` without the type segment still acts on both ends together (pair-wise accept/reject, the usual move-revision intent). Use the @id= form in scripts; positional indexes shift after accept/reject. Both `get /revision[@id=N]` and `set /revision[@id=N] --prop revision.action=…` accept the same path. Two disjoint property namespaces: `revision.type=ins|del|format|moveFrom|moveTo` creates a tracked change (used with add/set on the host run/paragraph/table/row/cell/section), while `revision.action=accept|reject` acts on existing markers via /revision selectors or native paths (/body/p[N], /body/p[N]/r[M], etc.). The two namespaces are mutually exclusive — mixing revision.action with any revision.type/.author/.date/.id key is rejected as ambiguous. Bare `revision=…` is no longer accepted. **Find + revision** (matches Word's Find&Replace with Track Changes on): `set <path> --prop find=PATTERN [--prop regex=true] --prop revision.author=NAME` infers the marker shape from the operation — `--prop replace=NEW` produces a w:del(old) + w:ins(new) pair, `--prop replace=` (empty) produces w:del only, format props (bold, font.color, …) produce one w:rPrChange per matched run, and paragraph-scope props (align, indent, …) produce one w:pPrChange per matched paragraph. The handler auto-allocates a fresh revision.id per marker, so `--prop revision.id=…` is rejected on find (would collide across markers). `revision.type=ins|del|moveFrom|moveTo` and `revision.action=…` are also rejected on find — the shape is inferred and the action verb belongs on the selector, not the creation context. `find` / `replace` / `regex` themselves are cross-cutting Set keywords (not revision-specific properties; not listed below) — pairing any of them with `revision.author` routes the find pass through the track-changes pipeline. Separate from `revisionNumber` on / (document save counter) and `trackChanges` on /settings (track-mode toggle).

operations.note: `set: true` covers the action verb (revision.action on a /revision selector or native path). `add: false` here means the *revision element itself* has no Add surface — tracked changes are created indirectly via `revision.type` / `revision.author` / `revision.date` / `revision.id` on the host run/paragraph/table/row/cell/section, where they read as `add: true` / `set: true`. Per-property add/set flags below describe behavior on the synthetic /revision node, not on the host.

action.description: Accept / reject verb. Lives in a disjoint namespace from the creation keys above; mixing with revision.type/.author/.date/.id throws. Walks matching elements in reverse document order so /revision[N] paths don't shift mid-batch.
action.readback: (action is a verb — not surfaced on get)

author.description: Author of the revision. Surfaced on `query revision` nodes and on host elements (run/paragraph/…) that carry an ins/del/*PrChange ancestor. Supply via --prop revision.author=… on the host element to create. **Run host is stricter:** `set <run> --prop revision.author=X` alone is rejected — without an action to attribute, the legacy fallback wrote an empty-snapshot rPrChange (recording no change). Either pair revision.author with a real format prop (`--prop bold=true`), or pass `--prop revision.type=ins|del|moveFrom|moveTo` so the wrap itself is the action. The same strictness applies to bare `revision.type=format` on Run. Non-Run hosts (paragraph/table/row/cell/section) still accept bare attribution and emit an empty-snapshot *PrChange — needed for dump→batch round-trip of table-level markers whose pre-change baseline can't be reconstructed. Also accepted alongside `find` (and `replace` / format / paragraph props) — the find dispatch wraps each match in the matching marker shape and attributes it to this author.
author.readback: revision author (read-only on the revision node)

date.description: ISO-8601 timestamp (read-only on the revision node). Supply via --prop revision.date=… on the host element to create.

id.description: w:id attribute. Stable across save; pair moveFrom/moveTo by reusing the same id. Supply via --prop revision.id=… on the host element.
id.readback: revision id (read-only on the revision node)

nativePath.description: Path to the underlying w:ins/w:del/w:moveFrom/w:moveTo/w:*PrChange element in document DOM terms (e.g. /body/p[2]/ins[1]). Use it to address the revision via the action native-path form: `set <nativePath> --prop revision.action=accept|reject`. Emitted on `query revision` synthetic nodes only.
nativePath.readback: DOM path to the revision element (read-only)

paraMarkDel.author.description: Author of a paragraph-mark deletion (w:rPr/w:del in pPr/rPr — the ¶ join is tracked as a deletion). Deletion counterpart of paraMarkIns.author. Get-only.

paraMarkDel.id.description: w:id of the paragraph-mark deletion. Get-only.

paraMarkIns.author.description: Author of a paragraph-mark-only insertion (w:rPr/w:ins inside the paragraph's pPr/rPr — the ¶ mark itself is part of the inserted change, distinct from a run-level w:ins wrapping content). Surfaced on paragraph nodes via get/query; on dump→batch the emitter rewrites it to bare revision.author on the synthetic paragraph + strips revision.type so the bare-attribution path defaults the type to ins.

paraMarkIns.id.description: w:id of the paragraph-mark insertion. Get-only companion to paraMarkIns.author/.date.

revision.beforeLost.description: Get-only signal: true when a *PrChange 'before' property snapshot cannot round-trip through dump→batch (the prior-formatting baseline is lost on replay). Lets the batch emitter warn rather than silently drop it.

sectPrChange.author.description: Author of a w:sectPrChange on a section (revision.type=format equivalent at section scope; surfaced as a distinct key because the snapshot lives in a different OOXML wrapper than rPrChange/pPrChange).

sectPrChange.id.description: Stable w:id companion to sectPrChange.author; preserved across dump→batch so the marker's identity survives the round-trip.

tblPrChange.author.description: Author of a w:tblPrChange on a table (table-properties revision — borders/style/layout snapshot).

tcPrChange.author.description: Author of a w:tcPrChange on a table cell (cell-properties revision — borders/shading/width snapshot). Separate from w:tcPr/w:cellIns/w:cellDel (= cell insertion/deletion).

text.description: text content for ins / content marker for del (read-only).

trPrChange.author.description: Author of a w:trPrChange on a table row (row-properties revision — row height/header/cantSplit snapshot). Separate from w:trPr/w:ins (= row insertion as tracked change).

type.readback: revision type (read-only on the revision node; create with --prop revision.type=… on the host element)

elements.note: effective.* keys (effective.size, effective.bold, effective.color, ...) are read-only inheritance-resolved values: the run does not set the property directly, but resolves it by walking the run/paragraph style chain up to docDefaults. Each carries a paired effective.X.src pointer (e.g. "/styles/Heading1" or "/docDefaults") so callers can locate the writing layer. effective.* never appears when the run has the corresponding direct value — direct always wins.

bold.cs.description: complex-script bold (<w:bCs/>). Word renders Arabic / Hebrew bold via this flag, NOT <w:b/>. Required for Arabic bold to actually render.

caps.description: render text in all caps (display only; underlying text unchanged).

charSpacing.description: character spacing (letter spacing) in points. Stored as twips × 20.

direction.description: run reading direction. Use 'rtl' for Arabic / Hebrew, 'ltr' to clear. Canonical key for run direction; matches paragraph/section vocabulary.

dirty.description: true when the run is flagged as dirty (rPr/dirty=1) — Word treats it as needing reflow on next open.

effective.bold.src.description: source pointer for effective.bold. Documented but not emitted today.

effective.color.src.description: source pointer for effective.color. Documented but not emitted today.

effective.font.ascii.src.description: source pointer for effective.font.ascii. Documented but not emitted today.

effective.font.cs.src.description: source pointer for effective.font.cs. Documented but not emitted today.

effective.font.eastAsia.src.description: source pointer for effective.font.eastAsia. Documented but not emitted today.

effective.font.hAnsi.src.description: source pointer for effective.font.hAnsi. Documented but not emitted today.

effective.italic.description: inheritance-resolved italic (read-only). Surfaced only when the run does not set 'italic' directly.

effective.italic.src.description: source pointer for effective.italic. Documented but not emitted today.

effective.rtl.description: inheritance-resolved right-to-left flag (read-only). Emitted even when 'rtl' is set directly so callers can compare direct vs cascade-resolved state.

effective.size.src.description: source pointer for effective.size — path of the writing layer (e.g. "/styles/Heading1", "/docDefaults"). Documented but not emitted today; only the resolved `effective.size` value surfaces on Get.

effective.underline.src.description: source pointer for effective.underline. Documented but not emitted today.

font.ascii.description: individual rFonts @ascii slot readback. On Add/Set use the unified `font.latin` key (which writes both ascii + hAnsi).

font.asciiTheme.description: Theme font binding for the ascii slot (rFonts/asciiTheme). Values: minorHAnsi, majorHAnsi, minorEastAsia, majorEastAsia, minorBidi, majorBidi.

font.cs.description: Complex-script font slot (rFonts/cs) — Arabic / Hebrew / Thai typefaces.

font.csTheme.description: Theme font binding for the complex-script slot (rFonts/cstheme). Values: minorBidi, majorBidi.

font.ea.description: East-Asian font slot (rFonts/eastAsia) — Chinese / Japanese / Korean typefaces.

font.eaTheme.description: Theme font binding for the East-Asia slot (rFonts/eastAsiaTheme). Values: minorEastAsia, majorEastAsia.

font.eastAsia.description: legacy alias for the rFonts @eastAsia slot. Get emits only the canonical `font.ea` key (for runs, paragraphs, and styles alike); use `font.ea` on Add/Set/Get.

font.hAnsi.description: individual rFonts @hAnsi slot readback. On Add/Set use the unified `font.latin` key (which writes both ascii + hAnsi).

font.hAnsiTheme.description: Theme font binding for the hAnsi slot (rFonts/hAnsiTheme).

font.hint.description: Font slot hint (rFonts/hint) — tells Word which slot (eastAsia / cs / default) to use for ambiguous characters (CJK digits, punctuation) so they render with the intended font. Values: eastAsia, cs, default.

font.latin.description: Latin font slots (rFonts/ascii + hAnsi) — ASCII / Western text.

highlight.description: Word built-in highlight color. Accepts named colors (yellow, green, cyan, magenta, blue, red, ...).

italic.cs.description: complex-script italic (<w:iCs/>). Same role as bold.cs for italic styling on Arabic / Hebrew text.

lang.cs.description: ComplexScript language tag (<w:lang w:bidi=.../>). e.g. ar-SA, he-IL.

lang.ea.description: EastAsian-script language tag (<w:lang w:eastAsia=.../>). e.g. zh-CN, ja-JP.

lang.latin.description: Latin-script language tag (<w:lang w:val=.../>). e.g. en-US, fr-FR.

noproof.description: exclude this run from spell/grammar checking.

rtl.description: right-to-left text (legacy alias of 'direction'). Get surfaces this as direction=rtl|ltr.

size.cs.description: complex-script font size (<w:szCs/>). Independent from the bare 'size' (<w:sz/>) which only sizes Latin text.

textFill.description: w14 text fill (color or gradient spec).

textOutline.description: w14 text outline 'WIDTHpt-COLOR' (e.g. '1pt-FF0000'). Width first, color second; '-' or ';' separator.

vanish.description: hidden text (not rendered, but present in the file).

vertAlign.description: vertical text alignment. Values: superscript|super, subscript|sub, baseline.

elements.note: Aliases: contentcontrol. Structured document tags — inline or block. Block-level SDT wraps paragraphs; inline SDT wraps runs.

alias.description: human-readable display name shown in Word.

date.calendar.description: date-picker calendar system (w:calendar), e.g. gregorian, hijri, japan.

date.fullDate.description: date-picker selected value as ISO-8601 UTC (w:fullDate). The actual chosen date, distinct from the display format.

date.storeMappedDataAs.description: date-picker XML-mapping storage type (w:storeMappedDataAs), e.g. dateTime, date, text.

editable.description: false when SdtContentLockingValues.SdtContentLocked is set on this content control.

format.description: date-picker display format string (e.g. yyyy-MM-dd). Date controls only.

id.description: OOXML SdtId value; source of @sdtId in stable path /sdt[@sdtId=N].

items.description: comma-separated dropdown/combobox choices. Each item may use display|value form (e.g. Draft|DRAFT) when display text differs from the stored value.

lock.description: content-control locking. unlocked (default), contentLocked (content read-only), sdtLocked (control cannot be deleted), sdtContentLocked (both).

placeholder.description: true when the control is currently showing its placeholder text (<w:showingPlcHdr/>).

placeholderText.description: docPart gallery reference for the placeholder content (<w:placeholder><w:docPart w:val=.../></w:placeholder>).

type.description: SDT variant. Only text/richtext/dropdown/combobox/date are supported at add-time. picture/checkbox are not implemented — create those in Word and edit via CLI. Type cannot be changed after creation.

elements.note: Sections are section-break paragraphs carrying SectionProperties. Canonical length readback is cm (via FormatTwipsToCm). Lenient length input (twips int, or 2cm/0.5in/24pt via ParseTwips).

colSpaces.description: per-column space overrides — comma-separated EMU/twips values, one per column. Surfaces when columns carry individual @space attrs.

columnSpace.description: space between columns. Canonical key. Legacy alias 'columns.space' still accepted on Add/Set.

columns.description: number of text columns. Add accepts combined form "N" or "N,SPACE" (e.g. "2,1cm"); separate space override via alias "columns.space".

columns.equalWidth.description: sectPr cols @equalWidth flag — true when all columns share the same width.

columns.separator.description: sectPr cols @sep flag — vertical separator line drawn between columns.

direction.description: section reading direction (writes <w:bidi/> on sectPr). Flips page side, header/footer anchors, and gutter for Arabic / Hebrew documents. Apply at section level alongside paragraph-level direction for a fully RTL document.
direction.readback: rtl (only emitted when sectPr/<w:bidi/> is present)

endnotePr.pos.description: section endnote placement (<w:endnotePr><w:pos>). sectEnd (section end) or docEnd (document end).

footerRef.description: path to primary (default) footer part. Convenience shortcut equal to footerRef.default when present.

footnotePr.numFmt.description: section footnote number format (<w:footnotePr><w:numFmt>). Restarting footnote numbering per page/section requires footnotePr.numRestart.

footnotePr.numRestart.description: section footnote numbering restart (<w:footnotePr><w:numRestart>). 'eachPage' restarts the counter on every page; 'eachSect' on every section.

footnotePr.pos.description: section footnote placement (<w:footnotePr><w:pos>). pageBottom (default), beneath (beneath text), sectEnd (section end).

formProt.description: section form-protection flag (<w:formProt>). When true the section's content is locked except for form fields. Bare on/off toggle; accepts none/off/false to clear. Sits after cols, before vAlign in CT_SectPr order.

headerRef.description: path to primary (default) header part. Convenience shortcut equal to headerRef.default when present.

lineNumberCountBy.description: line numbering interval (every Nth line gets a number). Companion to lineNumbers; only emitted when > 1.

lineNumberDistance.description: gutter spacing in twips between the line-number column and the body text (<w:lnNumType w:distance>). Sibling attribute to lineNumberCountBy/lineNumberStart; surfaced independently so a section that carries only @distance round-trips. Does not by itself imply a restart mode.

pageNumFmt.description: page-number numeric format (writes w:pgNumType/@w:fmt). Common: decimal / lowerRoman / upperRoman / lowerLetter / upperLetter. Locale-specific: hindiNumbers / hindiVowels / arabicAlpha / arabicAbjad / thaiCounting / chineseCounting / japaneseCounting / koreanCounting / ideographDigital. Use 'hindiNumbers' for Indic-Arabic numerals (٠١٢٣) common in Arabic documents.

pageStart.description: starting page number for the section (writes w:pgNumType/@w:start). Use 'none'/'off' to clear.

paperSrc.first.description: printer paper-source bin for the first page of the section (<w:paperSrc w:first>). Integer printer-tray id (0–65535). Sits after pgMar, before pgBorders in CT_SectPr order.

paperSrc.other.description: printer paper-source bin for the remaining pages of the section (<w:paperSrc w:other>). Integer printer-tray id (0–65535). Companion to paperSrc.first.

pgBorders.description: page-border shorthand. 'box' materialises a single 4pt auto-colour border on all four sides; 'none' strips pgBorders. Get/dump surfaces existing page borders as the per-side pgBorders.<top|left|bottom|right> keys (with full val/sz/color/space) instead, so the shorthand is input-only.

pgBorders.bottom.description: bottom page border, STYLE[;SIZE[;COLOR[;SPACE]]]. See pgBorders.top.
pgBorders.bottom.readback: STYLE on the key, SIZE/COLOR/SPACE on .sz/.color/.space sub-keys

pgBorders.display.description: pgBorders @display — which pages of the section the page border appears on (all pages, first page only, or every page except the first). Surfaced on get only when present.

pgBorders.left.description: left page border, STYLE[;SIZE[;COLOR[;SPACE]]]. See pgBorders.top.
pgBorders.left.readback: STYLE on the key, SIZE/COLOR/SPACE on .sz/.color/.space sub-keys

pgBorders.offsetFrom.description: pgBorders @offsetFrom — whether the border is measured from the page edge ('page') or the text margin ('text', the OOXML default). Surfaced on get only when present.

pgBorders.right.description: right page border, STYLE[;SIZE[;COLOR[;SPACE]]]. See pgBorders.top.
pgBorders.right.readback: STYLE on the key, SIZE/COLOR/SPACE on .sz/.color/.space sub-keys

pgBorders.top.description: top page border, STYLE[;SIZE[;COLOR[;SPACE]]] (SIZE in eighths-of-pt or a unit-qualified length; SPACE in points-from-edge twips). Mirrors the paragraph pbdr.* / table border.* per-side value form.
pgBorders.top.readback: STYLE on the key, SIZE/COLOR/SPACE on .sz/.color/.space sub-keys

pgBorders.zOrder.description: pgBorders @zOrder — whether the page border is drawn IN FRONT of ('front', the OOXML default) or BEHIND ('back') the page text. Surfaced on get only when present.

rtlGutter.description: places the binding gutter on the right side (writes <w:rtlGutter/> on sectPr). Used together with direction=rtl for Arabic/Hebrew layouts.
rtlGutter.readback: true (only emitted when sectPr/<w:rtlGutter/> is present)

textDirection.description: section page text flow direction (<w:textDirection>), East-Asian vertical layout. tbRl is the common vertical (top-to-bottom, columns right-to-left) page flow. Accepts none/off to clear. Distinct from the cell-level textDirection in tcPr.

titlePage.description: enable distinct first-page header/footer for the section (writes <w:titlePg/>).

type.description: section break type. Only applies to mid-document sections at /section[N]; the body-level path / refers to the final section which has no break type, and Set rejects 'type'/'break' there with an actionable error pointing at /section[N].

vAlign.description: vertical alignment of page content (<w:vAlign>). 'center' vertically centers the body; 'both' justifies top-to-bottom. Accepts none/off to clear.

elements.note: Style type defaults to paragraph. 'id' must be unique in styles.xml; duplicate id rejected if explicit, else auto-suffixed. Built-in ids (Normal, Heading1..9, Title, Subtitle, Quote, IntenseQuote, ListParagraph, NoSpacing, TOCHeading) bypass the customStyle=true flag. Path forms /style[@name=NAME] and /style[N] are NOT supported — only /styles/StyleId resolves; navigation does not handle a bare 'style' top-level segment.

autoSpaceDE.description: auto spacing between East-Asian and Latin text. Applied to the style's pPr.

autoSpaceDN.description: auto spacing between East-Asian text and numbers. Applied to the style's pPr.

basedOn.description: parent style id to inherit from. Must be an existing w:styleId (not display name). Inherited properties are overridden by properties defined on this style.

basedOn.path.description: resolved path to the parent style node (get-only). Shortcut: use basedOn to Set.

bidi.description: right-to-left paragraph direction. Applied to the style's pPr.

caps.description: all-caps display on the style's rPr.

charSpacing.description: character spacing (letter-spacing) on the style's rPr.

color.description: font color. Accepts #RRGGBB, RRGGBB, named colors (red, blue…), rgb(r,g,b), or 3-char shorthand (F00).

contextualSpacing.description: suppress spacing between paragraphs of the same style.

customStyle.description: mark the style as user-authored rather than one of Word's built-ins (<w:customStyle/>). Carried verbatim through dump->batch so a built-in style renamed to a short id (Normal->'a') is NOT mis-flagged as custom on rebuild. Omitted from `add`/`set` falls back to inferring built-in-ness from the styleId.

direction.description: Paragraph reading direction (Arabic / Hebrew). 'rtl' writes <w:bidi/> on the style pPr; equivalent to bidi=true in canonical form.

dstrike.description: double-line strikethrough on the style's rPr.

effective.alignment.description: resolved paragraph alignment after walking basedOn → linked → docDefaults.

effective.alignment.src.description: source pointer for effective.alignment (style id chain).

effective.highlight.description: resolved highlight color name (yellow|green|cyan|...) inherited from the style chain.

effective.strike.description: true when strike-through is inherited from the style chain.

font.asciiTheme.description: Theme font binding for the style's ascii slot (rFonts/asciiTheme). Values: minorHAnsi, majorHAnsi, minorEastAsia, majorEastAsia, minorBidi, majorBidi.

font.cs.description: Complex-script font slot (rFonts/cs) for the style — Arabic / Hebrew / Thai typefaces.

font.csTheme.description: Theme font binding for the style's complex-script slot (rFonts/cstheme). Values: minorBidi, majorBidi.

font.ea.description: East-Asian font slot (rFonts/eastAsia) for the style — Chinese / Japanese / Korean typefaces.

font.eaTheme.description: Theme font binding for the style's East-Asia slot (rFonts/eastAsiaTheme). Values: minorEastAsia, majorEastAsia.

font.hAnsiTheme.description: Theme font binding for the style's hAnsi slot (rFonts/hAnsiTheme).

highlight.description: highlight color (yellow, green, cyan, magenta, blue, red, darkBlue, darkCyan, darkGreen, darkMagenta, darkRed, darkYellow, darkGray, lightGray, black, white, none).

id.description: w:styleId (unique, immutable identity). Aliases fall through to 'name' when 'id' is omitted. Renaming after Add would require rewriting every paragraph/run/basedOn reference in the document; not supported.

ilvl.description: list level (0-8) for the style-borne numPr.

keepLines.description: keep all lines of this paragraph together on one page.

keepNext.description: keep this paragraph on the same page as the next.

kinsoku.description: kinsoku (CJK line-break rules) toggle. Applied to the style's pPr.

lineRule.description: line spacing rule paired with lineSpacing. 'auto' = multiplier, 'exact' = exact fixed height, 'atLeast' = minimum height (lines may grow to fit tall content). Applied to the style's pPr.

lineSpacing.description: line spacing — multiplier (1.5x, 150%) or fixed (18pt). Applied to the style's pPr.

linked.description: linked-style pair: a paragraph style references its companion character style (or vice versa). Word UI: 'Linked (paragraph and character)'. Value is the partner styleId; must exist in styles.xml.

locked.description: lock the style against use when document formatting protection is active (<w:locked/>).

name.description: display name. Defaults to 'id' when omitted.

numId.description: numbering instance ID this style references. Paragraphs using --prop style=<id> inherit numbering through ResolveNumPrFromStyle without their own numPr — the canonical multi-level outline pattern (Heading1..9). Requires the numId to already exist in /numbering.

outlineLvl.description: outline level (0-9, 0=Heading 1). Drives TOC and Navigator. Applied to the style's pPr.

overflowPunct.description: allow punctuation to hang outside the text margin (CJK). Applied to the style's pPr.

pageBreakBefore.description: force a page break before each paragraph using this style.

pbdr.description: paragraph border. Sub-keys: pbdr.top / pbdr.bottom / pbdr.left / pbdr.right / pbdr.between / pbdr.bar / pbdr.all. Value form: 'style:size:color' (e.g. 'single:6:#FF0000'). Set-only — Get does not surface paragraph borders on the style today.

qFormat.description: mark as a 'primary' (quick) style shown in Word's Styles gallery (<w:qFormat/>). The default Normal style carries this; without round-tripping it the gallery designation is lost.

rtl.description: right-to-left run layout on the style's rPr.

semiHidden.description: hide the style from the main Styles gallery while keeping it usable (<w:semiHidden/>). Usually paired with unhideWhenUsed.

shading.description: background shading fill color on the style's rPr (or pPr for paragraph styles).

size.description: font size. Accepts bare number or pt-suffixed.

smallCaps.description: small-caps display on the style's rPr.

snapToGrid.description: snap to document grid for CJK layout. Applied to the style's pPr. Add/Set only — Get does not surface this back today.

spaceAfterAuto.description: w:spacing @afterAutospacing — automatic spacing toggle for the space AFTER paragraphs using this style. See spaceBeforeAuto.

spaceBeforeAuto.description: w:spacing @beforeAutospacing — Word's 'automatic spacing between paragraphs of the same style' toggle for the space BEFORE paragraphs using this style (e.g. the built-in 'Normal (Web)' style). Surfaced on get only when present.

strike.description: single-line strikethrough on the style's rPr.

suppressAutoHyphens.description: disable automatic hyphenation in this style. Add/Set only — Get does not surface this back today.

suppressLineNumbers.description: exclude this paragraph style from line numbering. Add/Set only — Get does not surface this back today.

topLinePunct.description: compress punctuation at the start of a line (CJK). Applied to the style's pPr. Add/Set only — Get does not surface this back today.

type.note: Style type is fixed at creation — changing a style's type after Add would orphan every paragraph/run that already references it. Recreate the style if you need a different type.

uiPriority.description: sort order of the style in Word's Styles pane / Recommended list (<w:uiPriority>). Lower sorts first.

underline.description: underline style (true/false, single, double, thick, dotted, dash, wavy, none, ...). Applied to the style's rPr.

unhideWhenUsed.description: reveal a semiHidden style in the gallery once it is first applied in the document (<w:unhideWhenUsed/>).

vanish.description: hidden text on the style's rPr.

vertAlign.description: vertical text alignment (superscript/subscript) on the style's rPr.

widowControl.description: prevent widows and orphans (single isolated lines).

wordWrap.description: allow word-break for non-CJK text inside CJK lines. Applied to the style's pPr. Add/Set only — Get does not surface this back today.

elements.note: StyleDefinitionsPart container. Add new styles here (see docx/style.json). Individual styles addressed by id: /styles/StyleId.

count.description: total number of style definitions in styles.xml.

elements.note: Only 'text' and 'width' are honored at Add time; every other property is applied via Set after the cell exists. Run-level formatting (font/size/bold/italic/color/highlight/underline/strike) is written to every run in every paragraph in the cell — and to ParagraphMarkRunProperties when the cell has no runs yet. Border value format is STYLE[;SIZE[;COLOR[;SPACE]]], e.g. 'single;4;FF0000'.

align.description: horizontal paragraph alignment applied to every paragraph in the cell (set-only; apply after add).

bold.description: bold applied to every run in the cell (set-only; apply after add).

cnfStyle.description: conditional-formatting bitmask (ST_Cnf @val) on the cell (tcPr). A 12-digit binary string — each '0'/'1' flags one region in order: firstRow, lastRow, firstColumn, lastColumn, oddVBand, evenVBand, oddHBand, evenHBand, firstRowFirstColumn, firstRowLastColumn, lastRowFirstColumn, lastRowLastColumn. Short binary input is right-padded to 12. Not a hex number.

color.description: run text color applied to every run in the cell (set-only; apply after add).

colspan.description: number of grid columns this cell spans. Aliases: gridspan. Adjusts cell width to the sum of spanned grid columns and removes now-redundant trailing cells in the row (set-only; apply after add).

direction.description: Reading direction (Arabic / Hebrew). 'rtl' writes <w:bidi/> on every cell paragraph, <w:rtl/> on each paragraph mark, and <w:rtl/> on every run; 'ltr' clears all three. Distinct from textDirection (which controls vertical/horizontal text flow inside the cell).
direction.readback: rtl when set, key absent otherwise

fill.description: cell background shading (w:shd @fill). Accepts a solid color — hex ('FF0000'), '#'-prefixed hex ('#FF0000'), named color ('red'), rgb(...) notation, or 'transparent' to clear. docx <w:shd> has no native gradient primitive: the CSS-style hyphen form 'COLOR1-COLOR2[-ANGLE]' is NOT accepted (native gradient cell fill is a pptx-only feature). A synthetic two-stop gradient is available via the semicolon form 'gradient;STARTCOLOR;ENDCOLOR[;ANGLE]', emulated with a shading pattern. Bare 'none' is not accepted — use 'transparent'. Set-only; apply after add.

fitText.description: enable w:fitText on every run so text is squeezed to the cell width (set-only; apply after add).

font.description: font family applied to every run in every paragraph in the cell (set-only; apply after add).

hideMark.description: w:hideMark — ignore the cell's end-of-cell mark when measuring the row height for autofit (the cell appears empty even though it holds a paragraph mark).

highlight.description: text highlight color. Mapped to Word's named highlight palette (yellow, green, cyan, magenta, blue, red, darkBlue, …) (set-only; apply after add).

hmerge.description: horizontal merge marker (w:hMerge — legacy form). 'restart' marks the leading cell of a horizontal span; 'continue' marks subsequent merged cells. Most modern docs prefer gridSpan; hmerge is preserved for round-trip with files that already use it.

italic.description: italic applied to every run in the cell (set-only; apply after add).

nowrap.description: disable text wrapping inside the cell (set-only; apply after add).

padding.bottom.description: bottom cell margin in twips (integer; 1 twip = 1/20 pt, e.g. 100 = 5pt). Raw integer only — no unit suffix.

padding.left.description: left cell margin in twips (integer; 1 twip = 1/20 pt, e.g. 100 = 5pt). Raw integer only — no unit suffix.

padding.right.description: right cell margin in twips (integer; 1 twip = 1/20 pt, e.g. 100 = 5pt). Raw integer only — no unit suffix.

padding.top.description: top cell margin in twips (integer; 1 twip = 1/20 pt, e.g. 100 = 5pt). Raw integer only — no unit suffix.

size.description: font size applied to every run in the cell. Accepts raw number (points), '14pt', '10.5pt' (set-only; apply after add).

skipGridSync.description: suppress the per-cell tblGrid synchronization side effect that normally fires when `width` is set. Used by dump → batch replay to preserve the source table's authoritative colWidths when individual tcW values disagree with the gridCol widths (Word renders by tcW; tblGrid is a layout hint). Set-only.

strike.description: strike-through applied to every run in the cell (set-only; apply after add).

tcFitText.description: w:tcFitText — fit the cell's text to its width (the tcPr-level toggle). Distinct from the run-level 'fitText' property, which squeezes each run via w:fitText.

textDirection.description: text flow direction inside the cell. Aliases: textdir (set-only; apply after add).

underline.description: underline style applied to every run in the cell (set-only; apply after add).

valign.description: vertical alignment of cell contents (set-only; apply after add).

vmerge.description: vertical merge marker (w:vMerge). 'restart' marks the top cell of a vertical span; 'continue' marks subsequent merged cells in the same column. Bare <w:vMerge/> reads as 'continue'.

width.description: cell preferred width (w:tcW). Accepts a bare twips integer or unit-qualified 'Ndxa' (Dxa), 'N%' (percent of table width), 'auto' (auto-fit, no preferred width), or 'nil' (no preferred width — distinct from a zero-twip explicit width). Get echoes the same unit-qualified form so the type round-trips losslessly.

elements.note: Virtual element — OOXML has no <w:col> child of <w:tbl>; the path is synthesized from <w:tblGrid>/<w:gridCol> + the per-row cell at column slot C. Same-table only for move/copy. Get/Set/Query at the column level are not supported (read width via /body/tbl[N] tblGrid or per-cell tcW). Insert is rejected when the column slot crosses a merged cell (gridSpan/vMerge) — unmerge first.

text.description: seed text inserted into every new cell of this column (one paragraph per cell).

width.description: column width in twips (or any twips-parseable length).
width.readback: n/a (column-level Get not implemented; inspect tblGrid)

elements.note: Row column count defaults to the parent table grid. height uses AtLeast rule; height.exact forces Exact rule.

cnfStyle.description: conditional-formatting bitmask (ST_Cnf @val) on the row (trPr). A 12-digit binary string — each '0'/'1' flags one region in order: firstRow, lastRow, firstColumn, lastColumn, oddVBand, evenVBand, oddHBand, evenHBand, firstRowFirstColumn, firstRowLastColumn, lastRowFirstColumn, lastRowLastColumn. Short binary input is right-padded to 12. Not a hex number.

gridAfter.description: number of trailing grid columns this row skips (w:gridAfter @val). Pairs with wAfter (the preferred width reserved for the skipped span) to shorten the row's right edge — produces a ragged/indented table edge.

gridBefore.description: number of leading grid columns this row skips (w:gridBefore @val). Pairs with wBefore (the preferred width reserved for the skipped span) to indent the row's left edge — produces a ragged/indented table edge.

header.description: repeat row as table header on every page.
header.readback: true when header, key absent otherwise

height.exact.description: row height in twips (Exact rule, cannot grow). Add/Set only — Get does not surface a separate exact-height key; inspect `height.rule=exact` paired with `height` instead.

height.rule.description: row height rule readback — `exact` when the row enforces a fixed height, otherwise absent (auto/atLeast).

wAfter.description: preferred width of the trailing skipped-column span (w:wAfter w:w/w:type). Same unit-qualified form as cell width: 'Ndxa', 'N%', 'auto', or 'nil'.

wBefore.description: preferred width of the leading skipped-column span (w:wBefore w:w/w:type). Same unit-qualified form as cell width: 'Ndxa', 'N%', 'auto', or 'nil'.

elements.note: Tables default to Single/Size=4 borders on all sides. Length props use twips (raw int) or unit-qualified. Data can be seeded via 'data' (semicolon rows, comma cells) or per-cell 'r{R}c{C}' props.

caption.description: accessibility caption for the table (<w:tblCaption> @w:val). Surfaced to assistive technology.

cellSpacing.description: space between cells in twips. Alias: cellspacing.

colBandSize.description: columns per band when a banded table style applies (<w:tblStyleColBandSize> @w:val). Default 1. Alias: columnBandSize.

colWidths.description: comma-separated per-column widths in twips. Aliases: colwidths.
colWidths.readback: comma-separated column widths in OOXML units

description.description: accessibility description / long-desc for the table (<w:tblDescription> @w:val).

direction.description: Reading direction (Arabic / Hebrew). 'rtl' writes <w:bidiVisual/> on tblPr (mirrors column order); 'ltr' clears it. Distinct from per-cell textDirection.
direction.readback: rtl when set, key absent otherwise

padding.description: default cell padding (all four sides) in twips. Add/Set only — Get does not surface the table-default cell margin today.

rowBandSize.description: rows per band when a banded table style applies (<w:tblStyleRowBandSize> @w:val). Default 1; e.g. 2 stripes every second row.

tblLook.description: conditional-formatting bitmask (<w:tblLook> @w:val, 4-digit hex) selecting which facets of a conditional/banded table style apply: 0x0020 firstRow, 0x0040 lastRow, 0x0080 firstColumn, 0x0100 lastColumn, 0x0200 noHBand, 0x0400 noVBand. The w:val hex is authoritative (Word also reads the decomposed boolean attributes; w:val wins). Get emits the source hex (e.g. 0620) so dump→batch round-trips every bit; the default 04A0 (firstRow+firstColumn) is only seeded when a table style is applied and no explicit tblLook is given. Decomposed facets also settable as firstRow/lastRow/firstColumn/lastColumn/bandRow/bandCol (or the tblLook.<facet> compound form).

elements.note: Aliases: tableofcontents. Inserts a TOC field (complex fldChar). Word rebuilds the rendered entries on open unless 'pre-render' is used.

pageNumbers.description: include page numbers in TOC entries (Add/Set use lowercase alias 'pagenumbers').
pageNumbers.readback: true if TOC includes page numbers

elements.note: Watermarks are inserted into the document header as VML/DrawingML shapes. Text or image variants supported.

image.description: image source for image watermark. Aliases: src, path.

opacity.description: opacity 0..1 float as string (e.g. 0.5). Verbatim VML attribute injection.

rotation.description: VML rotation in degrees (0-360). Defaults to 315 (diagonal). Accepts negative input: -45 is stored and read back as 315.

size.description: font size for text watermark (pt). Default 1pt (auto-fit).
